issue_title: Ability to define number of CPU/GPUs to be used when creating the TF Session
issue_body: Tensorflow provides means to specify the number of CPU/GPUs to be used for each Session. Otherwise, it will try to use all CPUs available in the machine. Currently tensorframes does provide any means to specify this information and it ends up tensorflow using all the CPU cores in the executor nodes. This is more problematic when an executor has more than one cores assigned which results in more contention. An alternative would be to set only one core for each executor but this is not optimal in mix workloads where CNN inference is only a part of the workload.

issue_title: Note in docs that several of these features are available in newer Python
issue_body:
I was using Monty because it was included with pymatgen. However, I think it is important to note that some features of Monty are available in Python >=  2.6 with a slightly different api. I understand that this package is compatible with Python 2 and 3 which is currently an advantage. Especially in the scientific community.

For example:

 - Starting in 3.2, there is support for transparent archiving and unarchiving of files via `shutil`. This does much of `monty.shutil`.

 - Starting in 3.2, there is support for temporary directories using the with syntax. `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory`.  

- Starting in 2.6, there is support for number of cpus `multiprocessing.cpu_count()`

issue_title: Add option to trim data values when converting SAS Data set to pandas DataFrame
issue_body:
It seems that character values are right padded when converting from the fixed width SAS data set to the variable with DataFrame.  This additional buffer introduces a loss of data integrity because of the unexpected structure.

A simple example for conversion is:

```python
sd = sas.sasdata("test")
df = sd.to_df() // or sas.sd2df()
```
Currently, a possible workaround is by stripping the data after the conversion:

```python 
df_trimmed = df.applymap(lambda x: x.strip() if type(x) is str else x)
```

Maybe, adjusting the API to allow an additional parameter flag that will execute trimming.  Something like _**trim=True**_:

```python
sas.sd2df(dataset, trim=True)
```

Thanks! 🍻

issue_title: Problem when using the DOCKER_HOST variable in combination with docker-compose and https://
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm trying to use docker & docker-compose with the DOCKER_HOST env-variable to control a remote docker-host.

at first I configured the variables on the docker client machine as follows:

    export DOCKER_CERT_PATH=/vagrant/docker
    export DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY=1
    export DOCKER_HOST=my-docker-vm.cloudapp.azure.com:2376

This lead to an error in docker-compose as discussed in this issue docker/compose#2634

Once I added the `https://` protocol prefix to the hostname the connection problem with docker-compose went away...

    export DOCKER_HOST=https://my-docker-vm.cloudapp.azure.com:2376

But with this format of the DOCKER_HOST variable now the docker CLI is complaining about the format...

    docker ps
    Invalid bind address format: https://my-docker-vm.cloudapp.azure.com:2376

I think this error is triggered here: https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/62d9964cc1881f6f3cd021594cd40fd80a8fc855/docker/utils/utils.py#L388

The code is not expecting to find two occurrences of double-dots `:` in the host variable, but for some reason it does.

PS: also the ambiguity of the two error messages in `utils.py` could be improved, since at L368 & at L388 both errors emit the same message, but for different reasons ;)

Thanks & Regards,
Wolfgang

issue_title: Can't create a service with multiple mounts
issue_body:
Hellow again,
While I was attemptiong to create a service with multiples line, I find an error in the api code:
In the file /docker-2.2.1-py2.7.egg/docker/types/services.py
Line 110 and following we have : 
```
            for mount in mounts:
                if isinstance(mount, six.string_types):
                    mounts.append(Mount.parse_mount_string(mount))
                    mounts.remove(mount)
            self['Mounts'] = mounts
```
Wich transform the required list `['/home/user1/:/mnt/vol2:rw', '/home/user2/:/mnt/vol3:rw', '/home/user3/:/mnt/vol4:rw']`

into the list :
`['/home/user2/:/mnt/vol3:rw', {'Source': '/home/user1/', 'ReadOnly': False, 'Type': 'bind', 'Target': '/mnt/vol2'}, {'Source': '/home/user3/', 'ReadOnly': False, 'Type': 'bind', 'Target': '/mnt/vol4'}]
`
Like you can see, the second element of my first list isn't parse into a dictionary.

The reason I found is because of the remove in the for each above.
After parse the first element of the list (index 0), it remove it. The second element goes to index 0 then end when the loop restart it goes the index 1. Then the second element is forgotten.
I will post a pull request

issue_body: We currently have [lots of places](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/allennlp/models/reading_comprehension/bidaf.py#L100-L118) in our model init methods that take 5 lines to do a simple dimension check, and it's onerous to add those checks, so they don't get added a lot of the time.  We should have a simple function to call that checks that two dimensions match, and throws a `ConfigurationError` if they don't.

issue_title: Add a tutorial for random non-standard things a person might want to try
issue_body: There are simple ways to do a lot of small tweaks in AllenNLP models, if you've set them up correctly.  Some of these ways are probably not obvious to someone not familiar with the code.  It'd be nice to have a tutorial that collected a bunch of these things and showed how to do them easily.  Tutorial title could be something like "What if I want to do...".  Some examples of things to go in here: "What if my text is in a language other than English?"  "What if I want to use two separate embeddings for the same words?"  "What if I want to use part-of-speech tag embeddings as my word representation?"

issue_title: Batch mode and paths?

Either

- forbid it by changing the guards for async-delete and async-transfer to accept only batch mode exclusive-or path

Here's what you can change:

```python
def process_template_response(self, request, response):

    return response  # FIX POINT
```

Thanks.

issue_title: Check the Kademlia pong results for hash of the ping
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

The Kademlia p2p pong is supposed to be the hash of the ping, according to page 4 of [this (PDF) paper](http://www.cs.bu.edu/~goldbe/projects/eclipseEth.pdf), which has a nice summary of the p2p protocol. The paper claims that without this check, "*pong* messages [could be] sent from spoofed IP addresses".

Here's where I expected a check:

https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/eb7138ae67caab09c16d886f59e54639ad97b522/p2p/kademlia.py#L425-L433

Is this implemented somewhere else, or some reason that we don't need the check?

### How can it be fixed

Check the pong result against the hash of the ping, failing the pong if it is mismatched.

issue_title: Use p2p.Server in trinity processes
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

We're currently running the sync processes (i.e. `FullNodeSyncer` and `LightChain`) directly in trinity, together with the `PeerPool` required for them to work.

### How can it be fixed

We should teach `p2p.Server` how to select the appropriate sync service and then use just `p2p.Server` in trinity, as it takes care of setting up and running the `PeerPool` and the server that accepts incoming peer connections

issue_title: coala-bears and conda
issue_body:
Hello, 

[coala](https://github.com/coala-analyzer/coala) is a code analysis tools that helps you fix your code and many style problems. It uses [bears](https://github.com/coala-analyzer/coala-bears) as plugins, (has over 65) to fix specific problems in many different languages.

However, these bears use external dependencies, some from `pip`, some from `npm`, even more. We are planning to decentralize our bears instead of holding them all together and make them `conda` packages, because we thought that would be awesome. However, for this to happen, we also need to make their requirements conda packages as well!

We were planning to make a script that would automatically upload commands that would install those dependencies our bears need and upload them to Anaconda using conda. But before doing this, we thought that maybe you would have something already that does something similar, or maybe you could help us a little in doing this if you're interested?

Thanks a lot for your time!

CC @sils1297, @justuswilhelm

issue_title: Strange representation of OrderedDict in iPython
issue_body:
I am not even sure if this is an issue, though I found this behaviour misleading and it actually differs from other environments I have used. I am using: **Python 3.6.1 |Anaconda 4.4.0 (x86_64)** and **IPython 5.3.0**. 
```
from collections import Counter, OrderedDict 

class F(Counter, OrderedDict):
   pass
words = ['hello', 'my', 'hello', 'world', 'absd']

F(words) # result F({'absd': 1, 'hello': 2, 'my': 1, 'world': 1})

```
While both **repr(F)** and **str(F)** return correct representation: **"F({'hello': 2, 'my': 1, 'world': 1, 'absd': 1})"**

I think the correct behaviour is that it should use **__repr__**, otherwise it's quite misleading.

issue_title: Source Deactivate Causes Too Many Arguments
issue_body:
I did my research and saw that this type of error was typically caused by having a `set` keyword in setting an environment variable. However, I had none. Finally, it dawned on me that between July 2015 and March 2016 that the way to deactivate was changed from:

`source deactivate py34`

to simply

`source deactivate`

When did this happen and how/why is it not make backwards compatible by simply ignoring extra argument?

conda build ...

issue_title: `conda activate c:\foo` activates non-existing environment
issue_body:
Somewhere between conda `4.0.5` and `4.3.21` the behavior of `conda activate` changed.

## Steps to reproduce issue:

- Create a dummy empty folder `c:\foo`
- Execute `conda activate c:\foo`

On conda `4.0.5` this would error out with `No environment named "c:\foo" exists in C:\Anaconda2\envs, or is not a valid conda installation directory.`. 
But on `4.3.21` it activates with no exception and creates a `Scripts` directory. 

I haven't tested on other versions of conda other than the two mentioned above.

issue_title: Coordinates and  duplication
issue_body:
Hello.

I have a question regarding how pysam calculates reference_end.

Here is a piece of my sam file:

NB502029:10:HLHHMBGX3:1:11206:6289:11333	163	chrX	48312853	60	75M	=	48312902	124	CCTGAAGTCGCGCCTGCCCCTCAGCCTGGGGGTCTGATGGCTCCGCAAGCGGGGGTCCAGCCCAACGACTGCCCT	AAAAAEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEAEEEEE/EE<EEEEEEE	X0:i:1	X1:i:0	MD:Z:75	PG:Z:MarkDuplicates	XG:i:0	AM:i:37	NM:i:0	SM:i:37	XM:i:0	XO:i:0	XT:A:U
NB502029:10:HLHHMBGX3:1:11206:6289:11333	83	chrX	48312902	60	75M	=	48312853	-124	CGGGGGTCCAGCCCAACGACTGCCCTGAGCTGCCGCCCGACCTCCTGCTGGCCGAGCCGGCTGAACCGGCGCCGG	EEEEEE/EEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEAAAAA	X0:i:1	X1:i:0	MD:Z:75	PG:Z:MarkDuplicates	XG:i:0	AM:i:37	NM:i:0	SM:i:37	XM:i:0	XO:i:0	XT:A:U
NB502029:10:HLHHMBGX3:1:12312:7639:19146	163	chrX	48312902	60	75M	=	48312984	157	CGGGGGTCCAGCCCAACGACTGCCCTGAGCTGCCCCCCGACCTCCTGCTGGCCGAGCCGGCTGAACCGGCGCCCG	AA//AA/EAAAEEEEAEA/<EEE<E/<///EEAE/EAE6/EAEE//EEE/EEE6//EAAE//EE/EEEEEEEE/E	X0:i:1	X1:i:0	MD:Z:34G38G1	PG:Z:MarkDuplicates	XG:i:0	AM:i:25	NM:i:2	SM:i:37	XM:i:2	XO:i:0	XT:A:U
NB502029:10:HLHHMBGX3:1:12312:7639:19146	83	chrX	48312984	60	75M	=	48312902	-157	GCCTGAGGTGGAGGCAGAGGGCCGGGCCGCCGCCGAGAGTCCCCGCAGGCCGCGGGGCCCGGGCCCGGGCGTGGG	EEA/<AEE/EA/EEAAE/EEEE/EEE<EAA/AA/E/AAE/AEEAE///EE/AE</EAAA/EAEE6EEAEEA/AA/	X0:i:1	X1:i:0	MD:Z:8A21A22T20T0	PG:Z:MarkDuplicates	XG:i:0	AM:i:25	NM:i:4	SM:i:25	XM:i:4	XO:i:0	XT:A:U

When I read this sam file with pysam (print read.reference_name + '\t' + str(read.reference_start) + '\t' + str(read.reference_end)) it gives me:

chrX	48312901  	48312976
chrX	48312901	48312976

I understand both reads mapped to reference_start 48312902 (equivalent in pysam to 48312901). But I don't get it how pysam calculated the reference_end. According to pysam I have duplicated reads but the bam used was processed with Pickard to have duplicates removed.

Could you please help me?

Thank you.

`az login`

**Environment summary**
Using 2.0.32 Azure CLI on Mac. I also tried on Windows and got the same result. I also tried using the Azure Cloud Shell and got the same result.

**Additional context**

issue_title: 409 Conflict in copy blob should include the x-ms-copy-id
issue_body:
Currently when you get a 409 copy error because the target of a blobservice copy_blob API call already has a "async" copy to that target in progress, there is nothing useful inside the message. I can't tell why it failed (My code didn't know someone else did an async copy to that particular target blob). When calling copy_blob I also can't predict whether it will be synchronous or asynchronous, so I don't know when my blobs might enter the "async copy" state where all other copy operations will fail until I explicitly abort the copy...

As a small fix, would be nice if the 409 Conflict which shows up as an exception contained the "x-ms-copy-id" which would make it so my code could intelligently handle that failure. Currently all my synchronous copy code has to explicitly catch the exceptions for scenarios they never could have caused, do a get_blob_properties() call, pull out the 'x-ms-copy-id', abort the copy in progress and then retry the copy. Note that there is no timing guarantee on things like the get_blob_properties and the "cancel copy", so my subsequent calls may (depending on timing) produce a new 409 Conflict error[1]...

[1] https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/azure/jj159098.aspx "Trying to abort a copy that has completed or failed results in 409 Conflict."

issue_title: strict=False doesn't propagate as expected
issue_body:
When one model is nested inside another, importing that model doesn't pass `strict` through as expected. For example:

```python
    from schematics.models import Model
    from schematics.types import StringType
    from schematics.types.compound import ModelType

    class Inner(Model):
        foo = StringType()

    class Outer(Model):
        inner = ModelType(Inner)

    def main():
        d = {
            'inner': {
                'foo': 'foo value',
                'bar': 'bar value',
            },
        }

        outer = Outer(d, strict=False)
        print outer
        print outer.inner
```

This fails with:

```python
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/private/tmp/schem/bin/main", line 9, in <module>
        load_entry_point('main==0.1.0', 'console_scripts', 'main')()
      File "/private/tmp/schem/schematics_rogue_field_issue/src/main/main.py", line 19, in main
        outer = Outer(d, strict=False)
      File "/private/tmp/schematics/schematics/models.py", line 240, in __init__
        self._data = self.convert(raw_data, strict=strict, mapping=deserialize_mapping)
      File "/private/tmp/schematics/schematics/models.py", line 287, in convert
        return convert(self.__class__, raw_data, **kw)
      File "/private/tmp/schematics/schematics/transforms.py", line 419, in convert
        partial=partial, strict=strict, mapping=mapping)
      File "/private/tmp/schematics/schematics/transforms.py", line 119, in import_loop
        raise ModelConversionError(errors, data)
    schematics.exceptions.ModelConversionError: {'inner': {'bar': 'Rogue field'}}
```

Instead, I would have expected it to successfully instantiate the `outer` object.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<tool_dependency>

                <action type="shell_command">wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_ANIMAL-1.0.1.jar</action>
                <action type="move_file"><source>TTSS_ANIMAL-1.0.1.jar</source><destination>$INSTALL_DIR/module/</destination></action>        
                <action type="shell_command">wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_PLANT-1.0.1.jar</action>
                <action type="move_file"><source>TTSS_PLANT-1.0.1.jar</source><destination>$INSTALL_DIR/module/</destination></action>
                <action type="shell_command">wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_STD-1.0.1.jar</action>
                <action type="move_file"><source>TTSS_STD-1.0.1.jar</source><destination>$INSTALL_DIR/module/</destination></action>
                <action type="shell_command">wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_STD-2.0.1.jar</action>
                <action type="move_file"><source>TTSS_STD-2.0.1.jar</source><destination>$INSTALL_DIR/module/</destination></action>
            </actions>
        </install>
        <readme>
Downloads effectiveT3 v1.0.1 and the three models from http://effectors.org/ aka http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/
        </readme>
    </package>
</tool_dependency>
```

Would become something like this:

```
#!/bin/bash
#House keeping: strict bash mode, etc
set -euo pipefail
#TODO - move to a temp dir, check $INSTALL_DIR is set and exists
#Start of conversion from XML recipe:
echo "Installing effectiveT3 version 1.0.1"
wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_GUI-1.0.1.jar
mv TTSS_GUI-1.0.1.jar $INSTALL_DIR/
mkdir $INSTALL_DIR/module
wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_ANIMAL-1.0.1.jar
mv TTSS_ANIMAL-1.0.1.jar $INSTALL_DIR/module/
wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_PLANT-1.0.1.jar</action>
mv TTSS_PLANT-1.0.1.jar $INSTALL_DIR/module/
http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_STD-1.0.1.jar
mv TTSS_STD-1.0.1.jar $INSTALL_DIR/module/
wget http://effectors.csb.univie.ac.at/sites/eff/files/others/TTSS_STD-2.0.1.jar
mv TTSS_STD-2.0.1.jar $INSTALL_DIR/module/
```

I would then be able to run this within TravisCI with the advantages that the install recipe is not repeated (``tool_dependencies.xml`` and ``.travis.yml``) and moreover I would actually be able to test ``tool_dependencies.xml``, e.g. https://github.com/peterjc/pico_galaxy/commit/243311cc50ab2675c5e6aa42524841e60e6602e8

`
    name = Attribute(attribute_name=name, attribute_value=value)

    name = Attribute.AttributeName('Cryptographic Usage Mask')
    value = CryptographicUsageMask(
        UsageMaskEnum.ENCRYPT.value | UsageMaskEnum.DECRYPT.value)

`
    name = Attribute(attribute_name=name, attribute_value=value)

    attribute_type = AttributeType.CRYPTOGRAPHIC_LENGTH

    attributes = [algorithm_obj, length_obj, name, usage_mask]
`

issue_title: xdist misidentifies failing test on teardown crash
issue_body:
Hi!

I'm seeing xdist flagging the wrong test when a crash happens at test teardown.
Given 2 tests:
- `test_a`, passes but crashes at teardown
- `test_b`, passes

xdist will identify `test_b` as failing.
I have been able to reproduce while using pytest-xdist and pytest-timeout.
Here are the steps I've been following:
- Test file:
```
import pytest
import time

@pytest.fixture
def timeout_fixture(request):
    def long_wait():
        time.sleep(20)
    request.addfinalizer(long_wait)

def test_a(timeout_fixture):
    print("hello")

def test_b():
    print("world")

def test_c():
    print("bonjour")

def test_d():
    print("monde")
```
- Run test with `--timeout-method=thread`, so pytest-timeout will kill our process and xdist will see it crashed:
```$ py.test -s -x -vv --timeout=2 --timeout-method=thread -n 1```

Result:
```
[...]
test_bug.py::test_a 
[gw0] PASSED test_bug.py::test_a 
+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++ Timeout ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
[...]
[gw0] node down: Not properly terminated
[gw0] FAILED test_bug.py::test_b 
Replacing crashed slave gw0
[gw1] darwin Python 2.7.10 cwd: /private/tmp/test
[gw1] Python 2.7.10 (default, Jul 30 2016, 19:40:32)  -- [GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 8.0.0 (clang-800.0.34)]
gw1 [4]
test_bug.py::test_d 
[gw1] PASSED test_bug.py::test_d 
test_bug.py::test_c 
[gw1] PASSED test_bug.py::test_c 
========================================================================================================================================================== FAILURES ==========================================================================================================================================================
________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ test_bug.py _________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[gw0] darwin -- Python 2.7.10 /usr/bin/python
Slave 'gw0' crashed while running 'test_bug.py::test_b'
============================================================================================================================================= 1 failed, 3 passed in 4.01 seconds =============================================================================================================================================
```

`test_b` is seen as failing but hasn't been executed at all.

I believe this is due to the way xdist handles crashed items. When a node crashes, it pops the running item and reschedule it on a new node. However, when the node crashes at teardown, the running item has already been dequeued.

BUCKET/images/COMPANY_NAME to store the images.  I have the browse path set to /images/ in settings because the subdirectory will vary by tenant.

Is it possible using js or some other method to dynamically change the browse directory to go down to a subdirectory based on the company name?

issue_title: Adding geojson properties to popup
issue_body:
I have the following code which uses this [geojson ](https://www.dropbox.com/s/jzs2yoiesf017hn/volcanoes_json.geojson?dl=0)file as input. 

```python
import folium
markers_geojson='volcanoes_json.geojson'  
map=folium.Map(location=[0,0],zoom_start=6,tiles='Mapbox bright')
map.add_child(folium.GeoJson(data=open(markers_geojson),name='Volcano').add_child(folium.Popup("A plain pop up string")))    
map.save(outfile='test5.html')

```
 As you see the code is simply displaying a static string. Is there a way to put marker properties values contained in the json file in the popup method?

issue_title: Conversation - Unauthorized: Access is denied due to invalid credentials
issue_body:
`watson_developer_cloud.watson_developer_cloud_service.WatsonException: Unauthorized: Access is denied due to invalid credentials`

I put my credentials in the Conversation example from `examples/conversation_v1.py` and got this error. It looks like `watson_developer_cloud/conversation_v1.py` might be hitting the wrong URL endpoint. (API docs say https://gateway.watsonplatform.net/conversation/api/v1 but the URL in that file does not have the "v1")

issue_title: The python-sdk is incompatible with a Permuation of Korean Laptop / Korean Code Page and Python 3
issue_body:
Although it is ok with a Korean Laptop / Korean Code Page and Python 2.
Talking to my Korean colleagues I have learnt that this is quire common for pip packages, which is why they stick to Python 2. The strange thing is that the other packages they installed (django and request), were both OK. 
Short term fix is a note in the readme. Long term fix is to modify the code that breaks using the Korean Python 3 permutation.

issue_title: powerline.segments.common.net.internal_ip doesn't work in macOS Sierra 10.12.5 even after installing netifaces
Inspect parameters from tornado handler method with coroutine
issue_body:
When used @gen.coroutine or similar decorator on handler method
```python
@gen.coroutine
def get(self, item_id):
```
inspect.getargspec function ([tornado plugin](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/apispec/blob/dev/apispec/ext/tornado.py#L83)) return decorated arguments, and then we got empty list
```python
args = inspect.getargspec(method).args[1:]
```
 And error when like this, when used function spec.add_path
```python
raise APISpecError('Path template is not specified')
```

It fixed If use more common function signature from inspect module,
For example
```python
args = list(inspect.signature(method).parameters.keys())[1:]
```

issue_title: Attribute name in fields
issue_body:
So I'm having an issue with using an attribute name in a field definition - seems like the expected behavior for getting the item using that attribute works, but then in the serialized data dict instead of ending up at the fields's name, they key in the serialized data for that field is the attribute name.

tldr, i'm expecting:
```
class MySchema(Schema):
    cancellation_policy = fields.String(
          required=False, attribute='default_cancellation_policy')

input = { 'default_cancellation_policy': 'foo'}
schema = MySchema()
output = schema.dump(input).data
output = { 'cancellation_policy': 'foo'}
```

but i get instead:
```
output = { 'default_cancellation_policy': 'foo'}
```

Anyone seen this or have any leads for a workaround? Thanks!

issue_title: Relationships aren't being serialized
Adding pickle.load and pickle.loads into cloudpickle namespace
issue_body:
I understand that you do not include an unpickler:
> [It does not include an unpickler, as standard python unpickling suffices.](https://github.com/cloudpipe/cloudpickle/blob/master/cloudpickle/cloudpickle.py#L9)

However it would be convenient if injecting the pickle `load()` and `loads()` methods into your name space, thus it can be used as a drop in.

You have `import pickle` surely it's almost a simple as:
```python
    load = pickle.load
    loads = pickle.loads
```

If I am honest, I am a little inconvenient to have to write:
```python
    try:
        import cPickle as pickle
    except ImportError:
        import pickle

    import cloudpickle
```
when I need to read and write pickled files/objects etc., and have to remember to use `cloudpickle.dump`[`s`]`()` and `pickle.load`[`s`]`()`.

Just having those functions in your namespace, just redirecting the work to `pickle` itself would make code cleaner, can therefore can be used as a drop in replacement:

```python
    import cloudpickle as pickle
```
This is common practise with an alternative pickler `dill`:
```python
    import dill as pickle
```
`dill` is great, but I have found that `cloudpickle` can pickle something I need that `dill` cannot.

I wouldn't mind even doing it myself if need be; however rather than doing the work (which may not be as simple as stated above), creating a pull request, and it being rejected because you do not want this feature; I thought I would ask first.

Is this something that you would be interested in doing yourselves, or accepting an pull request for? or am I barking up the wrong tree?

issue_title: Z3 loops and ignores soft time-out
issue_body:
In the attached file, the variables `s#0` and `t#0` are set up in the same way. Trying to prove something about `s#0` immediately returns with a result, whereas changing the file to try to prove the same thing for `t#0` causes Z3 (version 4.4.1 or 4.4.0) to immerse itself in its toil to the extent that it even ignores the soft timeout.

To repro:
* See the end of the attached input file.  Choose either the marked `s#0` line or the marked `t#0` line.  Then run z3.exe version 4.4.1 or 4.4.0 on the input file.
[dd.smt2](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/37424/dd.smt2.txt)

Notes:
* I am, of course, aware of that minute changes in the input can cause dramatic changes in Z3's behavior. However, the fact that the soft timeout gets ignored here smells to me like some bug.
* I have distilled this file from a larger one with more axioms, where the `s#0` line resulted in a Valid (that is, unsat) query. In that case, using the `t#0` line caused Z3 never to come back at all.  With the attached file, Z3 does terminate on the `t#0` line, but after about 25 seconds, even with `-t:3000`.  I'd be happy to furnish this larger input file, if you want it.

issue_title: `ImageSeries.dimensions` should be `int`
issue_body:
from the tutorial:
```python
image_series = TwoPhotonSeries(name='test_iS', source='Ca2+ imaging example', dimension=[2],
                               external_file=['images.tiff'], imaging_plane=imaging_plane,
                               starting_frame=[0], format='tiff', timestamps=list())
```

I think `dimensions` should be an `int`. It is `int` in [nwb-schema](https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/nwb-schema/blob/76fdcc0051568ed3ace184da751dc20d2d202407/core/nwb.image.yaml):

```yaml
    - rank
    doc: Number of pixels on x, y, (and z) axes.
    dtype: int32
    name: dimension
    quantity: '?'
    shape:
    - null
```

issue_title: Strlen SimProcedure type error
issue_body:
VFG creation of a function containing a `strcpy()` call throws a `TypeError` exception inside the `strlen()` sim procedure.  The `strcpy()` sim procedure internally uses the `strlen()` sim procedure.  The issue, I think, stems from re-assigning a list variable the result of `max()`, which returns a single element.

Re-create the exception with what is documented in https://github.com/angr/angr/issues/27#issuecomment-397737270

Proposed patch/diff attached.
[strlen-patch.txt](https://github.com/angr/angr/files/2111562/strlen-patch.txt)

issue_title: Enhanced feature: Allow to derive the band integrated radiance
issue_body:
#### Code Sample, a minimal, complete, and verifiable piece of code

```
from pyspectral.radiance_tb_conversion import RadTbConverter
from pyspectral.utils import debug_on
debug_on()
modis = RadTbConverter('EOS-Aqua', 'modis', '20')
res = modis.tb2radiance(281., '20', lut=False)

```
#### Problem description

When the above code is run, the spectral radiance for the MODIS band 20 is derived.
One issue is that it should actually not be needed to pass the band to the method. It should
actually not be allowed to derive the radiance for another band.

But, my main issue, is that it should also be possible to derive the band integrated radiance.
This can be derived by not dividing with the integral of the relative spectral response for the given 
band. So something like this:
```
modis = RadTbConverter('EOS-Aqua', 'modis', '20')
res = modis.tb2radiance(281., lut=False, normalized=False)

Thank you for reporting an issue !

issue_body:
Right now the `LHCb.LocalSite` is wrongly defined in `/cvmfs/lhcb.cern.ch/lib/GangaConfig/config/6-3-0/GangaLHCb.ini`:
```

`git grep` reveals a couple of examples to be changed.

The workaround is of course to add `LocalSite=LCG.CERN.cern` under '[LHCb]` in `.gangarc`.

issue_title: PCA: too much memory
issue_body:
In the manual (29.14.2. Cartesian covariance matrix calculation and projection (PCA)

```bash
# Step one. Generate average structure.
# RMS-Fit to first frame to remove global translation/rotation.
parm data/tz2.parm7
trajin data/tz2.nc
rms first !@H=
average crdset AVG
run
# Step two. RMS-Fit to average structure. Calculate covariance matrix.
# Save the fit coordinates.
rms ref AVG !@H=
matrix covar name MyMatrix !@H=
createcrd CRD1
run
# Step three. Diagonalize matrix.
runanalysis diagmatrix MyMatrix vecs 2 name MyEvecs
# Step four. Project saved fit coordinates along eigenvectors 1 and 2
crdaction CRD1 projection evecs MyEvecs !@H= out project.dat beg 1 end 2
```

The `createcrd CRD1` step used up my RAM. Is the `createcrd` idiom for `projection`?

issue_title: Matches from Get match history returns 11 participants
issue_body:
In pdb `len(matchlist[0].participants)` returns an unexpected 11. In fact it returns 11 for all matches in the list.

summoner_name = chowdog
region=NA
champions =[119] #draven

Some things I've tried:
- I've checked the json returned from riot and it returns the correct number of participants. 
- grabbing the match by itself using the match_id returns the correct result.
- Now when i try the same endpoint call but with summoner_name=aphromoo instead, it works as expected, retuning only 10 participants.

Seems like this bug affects only some players, and has something to do with get_match_history

```
@require_http_methods(["GET"])
def get_current_match_details(request):
    summoner_name = request.GET['summoner_name']
    region = request.GET['region']
    champion_id = int(request.GET['champion_id'])

    s = cass.get_summoner(name=summoner_name, region=region)
    if s.exists:
        matchlist = cass.get_match_history(summoner=s, region=region, champions=[champion_id], begin_index=0, end_index=20)

        pdb.set_trace()
    else:
        return HttpResponse(status=404)
```

So it turns out that the first participant is a dummy, printing out the json gives:
```
(Pdb) matchlist[0].participants[0].to_json()
'{"accountId": 226913554, "id": 1, "stats": {"role": "DUO_CARRY", "lane": "BOTTOM"}, "championId": 119, "currentPlatformId": "NA1"}'
```

issue_title: Use Graphene without Relay queries
performance image modules: inception
issue_body:
Hi all, 
this is more of a question than an issue, but since it's really about the implementation of the module, this seems to be the right place to ask. 

I'm currently using a module that generates a feature vector based on inception v3 (https://www.tensorflow.org/hub/modules/google/imagenet/inception_v3/feature_vector/1 ) which is based on the tf-slim implementation. 
When I run this I get a about 9 global steps/second which is much better than when I just run the tf-slim network (that one runs at 3 steps/second). I do like this gain in performance, but I would like to understand how this is achieved, is this because you used the more optimized tf-layers compared to tf-slim layers? Or do you not calculate everything that tf-slim inception calculates? I know that they also calculate/train aux-logits for example. In their code I also see that they have an additional average pooling part, is that also part of the hub module? 

So perhaps to clarify there are two parts of my question:
1. What is actually considered as the embedding in this module?
2. What causes the significant difference in performance, are these just optimizations on implementation or  should I expect some accuracy impact as well?.

Thanks for your help in understanding these results

issue_title: There is no check on the channel value of otLinkSetChannel
issue_body:
The value provided to `otLinkSetChannel` is directly passed to `Mac::SetChannel`. In this function `OT_ERROR_NONE` is always returned. Therefore, `otLinkSetChannel` will never generate an error when an invalid channel number is provided.

wpanctl in wpantund will default the channel to 0
`target_network.channel = strtol(optarg, NULL, 0);` (wpantund/src/wpanctl/tool-cmd-join.c:97)
which eventually gets passed to `otLinkSetChannel`

issue_title: atrous_conv_2d failing if rate != 1
issue_body:
Hi,

there seems to be a bug in the atrous_conv_2d if the rate is not 1. The rate gets converted to a list with 4 entries by utils.autoformat_kernel_2d (https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/blob/master/tflearn/layers/conv.py#L338).  This leads to a "unsupported operand type" error when the tensorflow codes tries to substract 1: https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/master/tensorflow/python/ops/nn_ops.py#L976

This error only occurs on rates != 1 because the tf code checks if the rate is one and finishes before reaching the critical snippet: https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/master/tensorflow/python/ops/nn_ops.py#L954

issue_title: certbot-auto initial install: some transparency, please?
issue_body:
We use certs a.o. to enhance privacy (making it harder to "snoop" our connections). But to ensure *our* privacy, it would be good to have some more transparency:

We're expected to run `certbot-auto` as `root`, and it then "will take care for the rest automatically". While this is nice and relaxing from the users' workload, I'd like to know in advance what gets installed where to. Some examples:

* which directories will be created, and which files placed where?
* will it install its "own stuff" just into the directory it's invoked from?
* would it "mess up" things when run from `/usr/local/bin` (i.e. by installing a bunch of dependencies there)?
* it looks like it always wants to install all its plugins, no matter what (e.g. I see no way to tell it I don't want/need the Apache plugin, so it doesn't need to install augeas). Is that so? The docs say nothing about it (or I've missed that).

Of course one could try to "reverse-engineer" those details from the `certbot-auto` script. But that's a) not feasible (considering many users do not have the technical background to do so) and b) should not be needed with an open-source program which is all about privacy and security.

**TL;DR:** Can we have at least a raw listing of what will be installed where to (raw e.g. in telling "Python modules go into `/some/dir`, without listing all their files) – and a way to tell which plugins we (don't) want?

This shouldn't be a rant, just reasoning :) Wondering noone brought it up before (or did I miss that as well?)

Thanks in advance!

issue_title: Performance issues on LogListenerTask
issue_body:
LogListenerTask should be improved to minimize the number of RPC calls done.

Inside Raiden there are 5 ou 6 tasks created using a LogListenerTask, each for a different event (e.g new channel, channel settled, etc). Each of these tasks poll `eth_getFilterChanges` every 0.5s (by default). This amounts to a lot of RPC calls and the events that are polled for happen very sparsely.

An initial suggestion would be to add one single `FilterPollerTask` that polls for all topics that log listeners want to monitor and delivers the events to the appropriate `LogListenerTask`. This should already reduce any number of event pollers to a single RPC call.

Later some form of caching should probably be employed.

issue_title: Create the API spec
issue_body:
This is an issue to track the creation of an `API.MD` document in the raiden repository. 

The Raiden API should be requests to a  *restful* webserver with various endpoints a very good discussion on which is going on in [this issue](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/issues/355).

The purpose of this task is to define all endpoints, and describe their behaviour in order to have a full spec for what the API should look like before implementing it.

Related issues:

- [API mother issue](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/issues/10)
- [Discussion on the endpoints](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/issues/355)
- [Discussion on the encoding of objects returned from/passed to the API](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/issues/357)

issue_title: making _host_agent a singleton class
issue_body:
This is brought up by #467 

Currently each task has its own `_host_agent` that connects to the remote host, submit jobs and check status. It makes sense to make this `_host_agent` a singleton class that handles all communications to the server. Even if it cannot combine job submissions, it should be able to combine all status checking commands. We might still need to login and check status for every 10 seconds, but this is already better than doing this for all 100 jobs.

issue_title: make it possible to include lambdas in tasks
issue_body:
currently, it is impossible for a task in [concurrently](https://github.com/alphatwirl/alphatwirl/tree/a106f1f8229ed5d6ac9a24e8198e8bafe0406891/alphatwirl/concurrently) to include lambda. this is because lambda isn't picklable.

issue_title: Extend the contents used for DataFrame filenames
issue_body:
This is based on a series of posts first made to Slack. But for the TL;DR:

* The TableFileNameComposer class currently creates filenames that contain only the binning scheme for the DFs
* Sometimes the same binning scheme will be used whilst other variables are changed to create a DF (eg, for systematics, where we change the weighting or corrections).
* I suggest we have TableFileNameComposer.__call__() receive the full DF config specification, such that any generic filename composer one would like to create has the full amount of information available to it.  So on [this line](https://github.com/alphatwirl/alphatwirl/blob/v0.9.x/alphatwirl/configure/TableConfigCompleter.py#L80), you would just pass in `ret`:
```py
ret['outFileName'] = self.createOutFileName(ret)
```

For the meantime, I have a hacky solution to my problem, which can be used instead of TableFileNameComposer:
```py
class WithInsertTableFileNameComposer():                                           
    def __init__(self, composer, inserts):                                         
        self.inserts = inserts                                                     
        self.composer = composer                                                   
        self.frame_idx = 0                                                         
                                                                                   
    def __call__(self, columnNames, indices, **kwargs):                                     
        this_insert = self.inserts[self.frame_idx]                                 
        suffix = kwargs.get("suffix", self.composer.default_suffix)      
        kwargs["suffix"] = "--{}.{}".format(this_insert, suffix)                   
        self.frame_idx += 1                                                        
        return self.composer(columnNames, indices, **kwargs)     
```

issue_title: 在用admin进行user和group添加和修改时会出现TypeError
issue_body:
前端显示：
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/27114287/43241572-8fcf3ce8-90ce-11e8-8d31-2303f881caec.png)
后台报错：
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/27114287/43241652-f6aee4cc-90ce-11e8-8dc6-9d9e90ace82c.png)
主要原因是，在/server/core/TransformMiddleware中对所有request格式进行了转换，所以django自带处理方法中会出现类型错误。我目前的解决办法是加入判断，屏蔽掉来自admin的请求，初步测试可行。

> class TransformMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
    def __call__(self, request):
        """
        Change request body to str type
        :param request:
        :return:
        """
        if isinstance(request.body, bytes):
            if not 'admin' in request.path:
                data = getattr(request, '_body', request.body)
                request._body = data.decode('utf-8')
        response = self.get_response(request)
        return response

不过这个解决方案只针对了admin，不知道会不会有别的类似的问题。

issue_title: Blosc silently fails with 2GB buffer
issue_body:
```
>>> import numcodecs
>>> import numpy as np                                                                                   
>>> a = np.ones(1024**3, dtype=np.int8)
>>> a.nbytes
1073741824
>>> codec = numcodecs.Blosc()
>>> x = codec.encode(a)
>>> len(x)
4505616
>>> a = np.ones(2 * 1024**3, dtype=np.int8)
>>> x = codec.encode(a)                                                                                  
Input buffer size cannot exceed 2147483631 bytes
>>> codec.decode(x)
# Freezes interpreter
```
It looks like Blosc is raising this error and it's not being caught [here](https://github.com/zarr-developers/numcodecs/blob/master/numcodecs/blosc.pyx#L317) because csize is declared as ``size_t``, and so cannot be negative.

Any thoughts @alimanfoo? Should be an easy fix, but it's not entirely clear how to test this. Do you know of any other ways we can provoke errors in blosc which don't involve mallocing 2GB?

issue_title: How to use GLSL lark parser on a QScintilla
issue_body:
Hi, nice to meet you.

Today I've discovered this really promising project and although I haven't tested it yet from what I've read I can say it looks very promising to use. Few days ago I was testing this little pyparsing [glsl parser](https://stackoverflow.com/a/39705506/3809375) and the results I was getting weren't too great for simple piece of code (~0.2-0.3s) so I wanted to give it a shot to lark to see whether I'll be getting faster results.

That said, I've opened this [question](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/50459326/qscintilla-glsl-lexer) couple of days ago but since I've discovered lark I was thinking that instead considering using a `QsciLexerCPP` subclass to address this problem (that is just wrong) I'd write my own `QsciLexerCustom` subclass using lark, more info about [here](https://qscintilla.com/subclass-qscilexercustom). But some questions arise to me:

 - Assuming I've got the lark grammar and built the AST, how would you create the styles with it on a QSciScintilla, could we come up with a little example to illustrate this? Could we come up with a 5-min example showing how to use one of the lark examples on a qscintilla?
 - I'll want proper glsl syntax highlighting on the qscintilla editor but I'll also want to show autocompletions of function definitions, function declarations, ... , will be that possible to achieve by using lark?
 - Is there any freenode IRC channel / forum / SO tag to talk/discuss about lark? I'm posting questions here assuming you'll have the tag "help_wanted" :)

Got few more questions but I don't want to make the thread longer, summing up, trying to learn how to use lark to syntax highlight text on a QScintilla component properly.

Thanks in advance!
Bruno
